title: Multimodal Agentic RAG: A Hybrid Framework for Intelligent Extraction and Adaptive Retrieval on Unstructured Data Streams
authors: D. Karthika Renuka, L. Ashok Kumar, Ms. N. Deepika, Ms. K. S. Thaarugeshwari, Ms. Sriya Santhanam, Ms. K. Tejasri, Mr. N. Harisindhu, Ms. P. S. Preethi, Ms. A. K. Sangamithra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In the context of enterprise environments, the proliferation of complex, unstructured data such as multi-column PDFs, as well as videos, is an impediment to proper information systems, especially since RAG systems have difficulty retrieving such data due to semantic flattening. In light of such challenges, the Multimodal Agentic RAG framework has been developed, moving beyond passive information retrieval towards active information orchestration. In the architecture, we have introduced three innovations: The Intelligent Extractor, which relies on Large Multimodal Models to leverage structural parsing and keyframe captioning; the Hybrid Chunking strategy, which upholds the integrity of visual objects; and the Agentic Orchestrator, which uses the Plan-Execute-Reflect workflow to self-correct the information retrieval in real-time. In the test conducted on the M2RAG and the enterprise data, the framework yielded high scores: 82 for context precision and 89 for multimodal faithfulness, far superior to the static baseline, validating the importance of agentic reasoning in information systems.

title: QuCumber: Wavefunction Reconstruction with Neural Networks
authors: Aditya Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As the field of quantum technology continues to evolve, accurate quantum state preparation is becoming increasingly important for various applications in materials, matter, and devices. Computational methods allow the reconstruction of quantum states from experimental data, but the increasing complexity of quantum systems demands continuous algorithmic innovations. In this paper, we introduce QuCumber, an open-source software package designed to reconstruct quantum states using machine learning techniques. QuCumber leverages restricted Boltzmann machines (RBMs) to represent quantum wavefunctions based on projective measurements. By utilizing RBMs, QuCumber efficiently generates new measurements that facilitate the calculation of physical observables, some of which may not be directly accessible through experimental data alone. This paper demonstrates QuCumber’s capability to handle both real and complex wavefunctions, providing valuable tools for advancing quantum state analysis in line with the growing scale of quantum systems.

title: AI-Driven Forest Fire Prediction for Disaster Prevention
authors: Mohammad Shkoukani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The increasing frequency and intensity of forest fires, worsened by climate change, present a serious risk to ecosystems, human beings, and properties. The early detection of these fires helps eliminate the impacts, using timely measures to check the situation and allocate resources. This research investigates the potential of artificial intelligence (AI) for creating an advanced predictive model for the occurrence of forest fires, which makes use of machine learning algorithms, and environmental data collected from various data sources including satellite imagery, meteorological observations, and sensor networks. This study seeks to construct a model capable of real-time precise predictions, with a special focus on identifying areas vulnerable to fire through the use of a combination of historical fire data and climatic, physical, and ecological datasets using a statistical analysis approach. The current model evolved from the above-mentioned features through systematic deep learning and advanced feature selection techniques by considering data sparsity and non-linearity in the forest fire datasets. Moreover, it aims at utilizing federated learning to enable collaboration over geographical limits without compromising the services of the participating institutions through the data that they choose to share. The preliminary results show significant improvements in the performance of the model as compared to the standard methods being used, thus, underlining the possibility of implementing an AI-driven initiative for disaster prevention and environmental management. This study adds to the existing literature on forest fires prevention, providing a clearer understanding of AI's role in predictive analytics for ecological conservation and public safety.

title: Overvoltage Stress Distribution on Transformer Bushings Under the Effect of Electromagnetic Transient Response
authors: Md Nazmus Shakib Khan, Md Abdul Ahad Juel, Abu Anas Nasim, Md. Zahidul Islam, Badhan Baishnab, Md Shahadat Jaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper investigates the overvoltage stress distribution in transformer bushings subjected to high-frequency electromagnetic transients, such as lightning impulses. A comparative simulation approach was implemented using COMSOL Multiphysics to analyze both real and perfect conductor configurations of the bushing. The study applies a lightning impulse waveform to evaluate electric field behavior along the bushing structure. Results show that conductor geometry and internal foil arrangement significantly influence field intensity, with localized stress concentrations emerging before the onset of dominant skin effect. Additionally, the output pulse exhibits a damping effect, indicating energy dissipation over time due to bushing impedance characteristics. These findings provide insights for improving insulation coordination, optimizing bushing design, and enhancing surge protection strategies in high-voltage power systems.

title: A Hybrid Edge-Cloud IoT Framework for Real-Time Plant Disease Detection using Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: K. Rama Gangi Reddy, K.S Thirunavakkarasu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The rising rate of plant diseases poses a significant risk to global agriculture, leading to reduced yield and financial loss. The current approaches of disease detection are through manual visual inspection, or processing using cloud images, with all methods struggling with latency, scalability and power consumption limitations; especially in remote rural areas. This work presents a new, light-weight, and effective IoT-based system called SmartSense+, which is aimed for real-time detection of plant diseases using hybrid edge-cloud infrastructure and multimodal data fusion. The system incorporates NodeMCU and ESP32-CAM modules for image acquisition at the edge level and for monitoring environmental parameters such as temperature, humidity, and soil moisture. By using adaptive thresholding and light-weight feature extraction processes on the edge device, only the necessary data is sent through MQTT protocol to a cloud server. The cloud layer uses a Decision Tree classifier with decision-level data fusion augmented features, which are combined with sensor data to enhance the prediction capability. Experimental results validate that the devised system provides a detection accuracy of 92.3% with considerably smaller data transmission size and latency in comparison with traditional cloud-only and TinyML techniques. In addition, the system facilitates real-time alerts and includes an easy-to-use mobile and web dashboard for farm surveillance. The contribution of this paper is a scalable, energy-efficient, and cost-effective solution optimized for small and medium-scale farms. The suggested hybrid IoT platform is highly promising for solving actual agricultural problems by facilitating early and precise detection of plant diseases to protect plant health and develop more sustainable agriculture.

title: House Price Prediction Algorithms and Model based on Machine Learning
authors: B. Naveen Kumar, Kunta Mallik Raj, Podishetti Uday Kumar, Chanda Gopi Reddy, Mohammed Zia Azeem
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This project presents a machine learning-based solution for predicting house prices with high accuracy. It utilizes advanced algorithms such as Linear Regression, Decision Trees, and Random Forest to build a robust and intelligent system capable of estimating property values based on a range of input features. These features include key elements such as the property's location, total area, number of bedrooms and bathrooms, and amenities like parking space, gardens, proximity to schools, and hospitals. The model is trained using historical real estate data, which helps it learn trends in the housing market and understand how different variables affect property prices. By analysing this data, the machine learning algorithms can uncover complex patterns and relationships that might be missed using conventional methods. Each algorithm adds value in its own way: Linear Regression provides a straightforward and interpretable model, while Decision Trees and Random Forests are effective in modelling non-linear relationships and feature interactions. This predictive system proves beneficial to a wide range of users in the real estate industry. Potential homebuyers can assess whether a property's asking price is fair, and sellers can identify optimal pricing strategies. Real estate agents and professionals also gain from this data-driven approach, which supports accurate valuations and enhances client services. In essence, this system brings more transparency and efficiency to property transactions, allowing stakeholders to make informed decisions backed by data analytics.

title: Spectral Fidelity Aware Hyperspectral Image Denoising via Unsupervised Triple Attention with Endmember Preservation
authors: Mohith Ram Sai Karthik Phalgun Kothamasu, Satya Sai Ramesh P, Arun P V
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) denoising is essential for improving data quality in remote sensing applications. While recent deep learning approaches achieve strong noise suppression, they often distort spectral fidelity, which is critical for tasks such as material identification and unmixing. In this work, we propose an unsupervised deep learning framework that introduces a novel Endmember Preservation Loss (EPL) to explicitly safeguard pure spectral signatures during denoising. The framework combines spatial-spectral feature modeling with both loss-based and architectural mechanisms for spectral consistency. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that incorporating EPL significantly improves spectral angle metrics while maintaining competitive PSNR and SSIM, enabling effective denoising without compromising spectral integrity.

title: PWM-Based Multi-Mode Converter for DC-AC and Isolated Renewable Energy and EV Applications
authors: Sowmyashree M S, K. Bindu Madhavi, G. Meenakshi Sundaram, A.Sagai Francis Britto, Deepa Mathew K, Natrayan L
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: A pulse-width - modulated (PWM) multi - mode converter has been developed. It makes the system capable to work in DC-AC mode or isolated mode and also it supports renewable and electric vehicle (EV) applications. This converter has three modes: separate mode, inverter mode and chopper mode. By changing between each mode, the reverse flow of current between the DC and AC terminals is controlled and the energy transfer is maximised. Due to the easy changeability of the operation mode, the voltage gain and efficiency are stable. The converter supplies 2000 W of power with an efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 9 {\%}}$</tex> and a maximum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 4 {\%}}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 3 {\%}}$</tex> total harmonic distortion (THD). It has two DC inputs, which are 100 V and 200 V. These inputs produce a constant AC output of 300 V Tests in grid connected and off grid environment confirms its effectiveness. Simulation and experimental data demonstrate the performance slightly better than conventional converters whose THD varies from 6.8% to 18.3% and its efficiency equals 95.7%. The PWM-based control allows a better thermal stability and electromagnetic compatibility, since less switching losses and also the uniform current flow are achieved. The design consists of a wide operating range, reduced number of passive components, and a one stage architecture. Chuck favors the ground source renewable hybrid system and EV charging station, which needs to be compact, bidirectional, and isolated.

title: AI-Generated Fake News Detection Using a Multimodal Approach on the MiRAGeNews Dataset
authors: Tamil Selvan V P, Vignesh S, Siranjeevi Rajamanickam, Balasubramanian Palani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: As social platforms evolve and AI-generated visuals become very common, the risk of fake news spreading increases, impacting public opinion and sentiments. Most of the current fake news detection methods relying solely on text overlook important visual cues, which can also be manipulated using AI. In this work, a multimodal fake news detection approach using the MiRAGeNews dataset is introduced that integrates both textual and visual features through advanced deep learning techniques. Social media now spreads stories faster than ever, and AI can create lifelike pictures and convincing captions that fool people in seconds. To fight this, an AI-generated fake news detector is built that checks words and images together. The model is trained on the MiRAGeNews set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2, 0 0 0}$</tex> posts made from real news and AI-made look-alike photos from Midjourney and captions written by GPT-4. The model tries three ways to mix what it reads and sees: a simple merge, Concatenation layer on RoBERTa + ViT acheives 0.985 accuracy that lets text and picture features interact, and a deeper bilinear blend. In every test, using both text and images worked better than using only one, helping to catch fake news more often. This tool aims to help people spot AIgenerated fake news and to keep public news and conversations honest.

title: Machine Learning in Electric Vehicle Consumption Modelling
authors: Kritika Paliwal, Pradeep Jha, Suman Kumari, Vibhor Vaish, Nikhil Vishwakarma, Agamya Bansal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The accuracy of electric vehicles (EV's) comes down to how accurate your simulation models are, that's the bottom line. Conventional models based on mathematics often fail when faced with nonlinear or chaotic problems and unfortunately there are many such problems experienced in real life on the road. In this paper we take another tack. We combine Machine Learning (ML) algorithms with conventional simulation models in MATLAB/Simulink so as to achieve much greater accuracy in predictions and refinements. Using some actual driving data we used Random Forest Regression (RFR) and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) to estimate key parameters, namely state of charge of battery, energy consumption, efficiency of motor, etc. The result of this ML-Simulink combination was that prediction accuracy was increased by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-12 {\%}$</tex> on the successful simulation models compared with basic physical models. In other words by infusing ML into the procedure we produced simulation models which were considerably more flexible than conventional methods when used in a realistic situations, besides producing a saving in computation.

title: Smart Irrigation Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Alaa Yahya Aref Ahmad, Mohammad K. S. Ma’aitah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Effective water management in agriculture is imperative for the sustainable and judicious utilization of resources, particularly in areas grappling with the scarcity of water. Conventional irrigation techniques that are inclined towards over-irrigating or under-irrigating can terribly degrade crop productivity and proper resource-saving. This paper investigates a new AI outlook to produce a smart irrigation forecasting program that optimizes water consumption based on real-time environmental data. In this project, the program is put to the test using combinations of weather conditions such as soil moisture zone, temperature, and humidity, the model predicts the best irrigation plan for various crop types. The prediction accuracy is improved by using a combination of supervised learning methods, and the model is trained on open datasets so that it can be scaled and customized to different crops and farming techniques. The results of the evaluation demonstrate the efficiency of the model, where water usage could be cut down by as much as 30% when compared to normal irrigation methods. This study provides the foundation for the deployment of smart irrigation systems that can be incorporated within farming practices, enhance responsible water management, and improve agricultural output.

title: Machine Learning Classifier Comparative Performance Analysis of Smart Mobility: Multiclass Traffic Congestion Prediction
authors: Mutaz Rasmi Abu Sara, Murad Zeer, Jawad Hasan Alkhateeb, Khaled Musa
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: City congestion has become one of the most urgent problems of the smart city ecosystem, which has to be addressed with effective and data-driven strategies to provide real-time classifications and remedies. It gives a detailed analysis of three machine learning classifiers, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) to predict traffic congestion (Low, Medium, High) based on a rich, multidimensional dataset that encompasses cyber-physical telemetry and social sentiment metrics. To create temporal features (Hour, Day, Month) that reflected cyclic patterns in the traffic, timestamps were used. Careful grid search optimization of models was performed followed by comparison by macro averaged (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score) metrics on stratified train-test splits. According to the results, Random Forest classifier has the highest test accuracy (96.07) and macro F1-score (95.15) compared to the Decision Tree (95.67) and SVM (91.47). Multiclass ROC curves and confusion matrices indicate a good discriminative ability as well as good generalization, in particular of important states in in terms of congestion, a good category such as the excellent category. The efficacy of ensemble techniques used in operational traffic categorization systems confirmed in this paper is useful in proactive mobility management and sustainable urban planning.

title: Real-Time Speech Enhancement for Dysarthria Individuals Using NLP and Deep Learning
authors: Sambasivarao Chindam, Gobika R, Vijeyasri T, C. Oswald
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Individuals with dysarthria often have trouble speaking clearly, which makes it difficult for others to understand them, especially in real-time conversations. This research aims to make their speech easier to understand using new methods from natural language processing(NLP) and deep learning.We fine-tune the CrisperWhisper (CRISPR-VSPR) speech recognition model on dysarthric speech datasets (e.g., TORGO), so that it generates transcripts from unclear speech. After that, these transcripts are processed into clear and natural-sounding speech using text-to-speech (TTS) models. This system creates a real-time process which converts speech to text and then back to clear speech which helps people with dysarthria communicate more easily.

title: Perception-Aware Intelligent Lighting Systems: Functional Safety and Control Architectures for Intelligent Vehicles
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper analyzes how perception-aware intelligent lighting is evolving into a safety-critical subsystem in intelligent vehicles. Using standards, peer-reviewed literature, and OEM/semiconductor technical reports, this paper synthesizes system-level control architectures of matrix LED and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>-pixel headlamps, their interfaces with ADAS perception stacks, and the associated functional-safety implications under ISO 26262. Reported diagnostic strategies for LED driver faults, supervision and watchdog mechanisms, and the influence of signal, power, and thermal integrity on long-term reliability are consolidated into a unified architectural view. We map representative lighting-related hazards to detection and mitigation patterns and derive a design checklist covering timing constraints, EMI robustness, current-control accuracy, derating behavior, and safe-state transitions. Remaining gaps in perception-linked lighting validation—including synchronization, explainability, and environmental robustness-are also highlighted. The results position intelligent lighting as an assurance-driven, tightly integrated element within next-generation autonomous and electrified vehicle architectures. Accordingly, the focus of this work is on system-level architectural and functional-safety considerations, rather than algorithmic development.

title: Hallucination-Aware, Injection-Resistant LLMs for Scientific Text Integrity
authors: Nimit Bhardwaj, Anvit Bhardwaj, Lavanya Garg
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Large language models are increasingly used in scientific writing and enterprise applications, yet remain vulnerable to two critical issues: hallucination and prompt injection. Hallucinations introduce fabricated or inaccurate information, undermining trust and reproducibility in generated text. Prompt injections manipulate model behavior by exploiting instruction-following tendencies, posing security risks in dynamic input environments. This paper proposes SciGuard, a unified framework designed to detect hallucinated content and defend against prompt-based adversarial manipulation. The architecture integrates token-level classification, contradiction-aware logic validation, and semantic prompt rewriting to handle both threats in real time. Experiments conducted on scientific and enterprise benchmarks show that SciGuard outperforms existing models across precision, recall, and F1 metrics while maintaining low latency. The results highlight the effectiveness of integrating factual verification and input control into a single system, offering a robust foundation for secure and reliable generative AI deployments.

title: UniRelease: A Unified Framework for Addressing Cross-Platform Mobile Application Build and Release Challenges
authors: Satyanarayana Gudimetla, Suresh Gangula, Sudhamadhuri Buhyavarapu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Cross-platform mobile frameworks (Flutter, React Native, Kotlin Multiplatform) enable shared-codebase development, yet build and release engineering remains fragmented and poorly understood. This paper reports a mixed-methods study drawing on 4,827 CI/CD-related issues mined from 12 open-source projects and three framework meta-repositories, survey responses from 156 practitioners, and semi-structured interviews with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3}$</tex> practitioners. We identified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 7}$</tex> recurring challenges organized into six categories. Motivated by these findings, we developed UniRelease, a framework addressing the highest-impact categories through three mechanisms: UniConfig (declarative build configuration), UniTest (differential behavioral testing), and UniCoord (release coordination). In evaluation on eight end-user applications-four from industry partners and four open-source-spanning all three frameworks, UniConfig reduced configuration drift by 68.5 % (95% CI: [62.1%, 74.9%]), UniTest detected 45.3% of platform-divergent bugs in retrospective analysis (95 % CI: [37.3%, 53.3%]) with prospective deployment confirming 83.9 % precision across 42 release cycles, and UniCoord reduced coordination overhead by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1 \times(\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>, Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}=2.72)$</tex> across 52 release cycles at four partner organizations. These results provide evidence that empirically-grounded tooling can reduce the engineering burden of cross-platform releases.

title: Design of a Low-Cost Smart Embedded System for Real-Time Alcohol and Accident Detection in Vehicles
authors: Dhananjay Satanure, Swapnil Biradar, Pranav Ghabade, Shenila Ansari, Aditya Chougule, Shailaja Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Road accidents caused by drunken driving continue to present a major global safety concern, resulting significant annual vehicle fatalities. Despite advances in intelligent transportation systems, most existing driver assistance and accident alert mechanisms remain functionally isolated, cloud-dependent, or lack seamless integration between alcohol detection, vehicle control, and emergency communication. To address these limitations, this paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost smart embedded system for real-time alcohol detection and accident monitoring within an integrated platform. The proposed architecture combines an alcohol sensor, accelerometer and gyroscope, Global Positioning System (GPS), and Global System for Mobile Communications (GSM) modules, all connected to an Arduino Mega. When the detected alcohol concentration exceeds a predefined threshold, the system automatically disables the vehicle's ignition and transmits an alert message containing GPS coordinates to the emergency contacts. In the event of an accident, collision impact sensed by the Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) triggers an immediate location-based SMS (Short Message Service) notification. The system was rigorously tested under simulated conditions to ensure reliability and responsiveness. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system provides timely alert generation and costefficient operation without reliance on internet connectivity. These findings demonstrate its suitability for scalable deployment in low-cost intelligent transportation, road safety, and emergency response applications.

title: Conversational AI in Medicine: Exploring the Role of Chatbots in Modern Healthcare
authors: Suprabhat Adhikari, Sarthak Patra, Rishab Shaw, Shweta Maurya, Tathagata Chatterjee, Jayita Pal
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The rapid development of artificial intelligence has had a significant effect on the healthcare sector and on the transformation of conventional healthcare practices. Among the numerous advances in the field of artificial intelligence, chatbots represent a feasible substitute to encourage patient participation and support professionals in healthcare. These sagacious systems are used for tasks such as rapid patient selection, personalized treatment recommendations and suggestions, initial diagnosis, and ongoing monitoring of the treatment pathway. In recent years, chatbots have played a significant role within the healthcare industry. This paper analyzes the state of the art in this field and considers some prospects in terms of many practical features. Moreover, this paper outlines several major drawbacks and difficulties restricting its widespread application. This article examines these factors, highlighting the challenges of integrating these approaches with traditional healthcare systems, concerns related to data privacy, limitations in natural language understanding, and the necessary integration mechanisms. In order to provide an honest picture, this paper provides a sample chatbot architecture for a healthcare application and demonstrates how it works using representative input-output interactions. The aim of this comprehensive overview is to inform researchers and practitioners about the present and future aspects of chatbot technology in healthcare services.

title: Vision Transformer Integrated with Shift Operation for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification
authors: Gnanaprakash V, Manu Raju, Sarasu R, Saranya N, Jeen Retna Kumar R, K. Harsha Sai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In order to diagnose neurological problems, organize treatments, and keep track of patients, it is essential to identify and separate brain malignancies from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) data. Brain tumors are responsible for one out of every four cancer-related deaths, making cancer the second most frequent cause of death globally. Prompt therapy can result from an accurate and fast diagnosis. The quick development of image categorization in recent days has improved Computer-assisted diagnostics. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are the most widely used neural network models for image classification. However, it has trouble in accurately identifying the lesion's focal location, its efficacy is limited. This paper develops a unique brain tumor classification model by combining shift operation with visual transformer to address the aforementioned challenges. Highly accurate brain tumor diagnoses are being made using deep learning techniques. Computed tomography, ultrasound, and X-ray imaging are less successful than MRI, which is the gold standard for machine vision-based brain tumor diagnosis. In medical image processing, deep learning models like CNN as well as sophisticated architectures like Vision Transformers (ViT) and hybrid models have shown impressive results. According to the result, deep learning models can greatly increase the performance and speed of diagnosing brain tumors, which will lessen the workload for radiologists. But in order to train the model, a lot of parameters are required. In order to address this, the vision transformer's attention mechanism is replaced with a shift operation. Its sole function is the exchange of a little percentage of channels across adjacent features; it lacks parameters and arithmetic calculations. Building on this straightforward process, ShiftViT model is suggested as a base network that substitutes shift operations for the attention layers seen in ViT.

title: Sentiment Analysis in Stock Price Prediction Using ML
authors: Rasha Abousamra, Thair Kaddumi, Shatha Mustafa Hussain, Mohammed A. Afifi, Gamal Khalifa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the realm of financial markets, predicting the future price of stocks is a very complex task that traders, investors, and policymakers cannot ignore. Relying on purely past price data is the approach that has been taken traditionally when stock movements were considered, but the market sentiment has been neglected. The integration of sentiment analysis with machine learning (ML) techniques in this study will be employed to increase the precision of stock price prediction. Text data from news articles, financial reports, and social media sources will be utilized to generate sentiment scores that will measure the overall mood of the market via sentiment scores generated by keyword analysis. The historical price data of the companies will be integrated into various ML models such as Random Forest, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Gradient Boosting with the sentiment scores thus formed. Experimental results show that models with sentiments analysis outperform models that rely purely on numerical data. Furthermore, this research has illustrated how fruitful it would be if financial decision-makers made use of the knowledge of stock price drivers that may be influenced by sentiment-informed ML models, thereby outlining the intricate nature of stock price fluctuations.

title: SecureEndoNet: An Encryption-Enabled, Transfer Learning–Based Approach for Endoscopic Image Classification
authors: Ahmmad Musha, Ahsanul Islam, Tonmoy Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: The use of deep learning models on endoscopic images for detecting gastrointestinal diseases presents a major privacy challenge, as it requires processing sensitive patient data and risks the unauthorized disclosure of personal health information. To address this issue, we propose SecureEndoNet, a transfer learning–based framework compatible with homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving endoscopic image classification. SecureEndoNet employs the CKKS scheme to encrypt client data during inference and utilizes a pre-trained VGG-19 model for feature extraction. Experiments conducted on the publicly available KVASIR v2 dataset, which contains eight classes, demonstrate that the framework achieves identical classification accuracy on both plaintext and ciphertext inputs (90.125%), with an inference time of 1.25 seconds per encrypted sample. Moreover, VGG-19 provides the fastest inference speed while achieving over 90% accuracy, outperforming other pretrained models including DenseNet-201, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, and EfficientNetV2B0.

title: Optimal Pre and Post-Silicon Validation Strategy for Vision-Centric ASIC Platforms
authors: Kranti kumar Thota
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The emergence of vision-oriented ASIC platforms requires stringent validation solutions to achieve functional accuracy and performance stability to the various real world workloads. Pre- and post-silicon validation stages are both important but limited due to the costly nature of computing them, and the time-intensive nature of their process. Conventional exhaustive testing methods find it difficult to effectively model corner-case behavior in complex vision workloads causing more design cycles and slow time-to-market. This work presents best-fit validation system to combine a gated dual-branch attention (GDBAtt) mechanism with the improved groundwater flow optimization (EGFO) algorithm to focus more on the critical test scenarios and fasten coverage-based verification. The modeling workload behavior, finding instruction and data patterns, and optimizing the process of pre-silicon simulation and post-silicon test vectors selection is modeled using a deep starlike neural network (DSNN). Performance on benchmark vision workloads has shown 8x (up to) improvement in simulation and test time, and 95.8 % coverage of edge-case behaviors. The findings affirm the subset sustains operational variety and identifies functional aberration, halving verification effort, cutting design periods and enhancing ASIC reliability.

title: Reconfiguration Technique for Enhancing PV Module Performance Under Mismatching Conditions
authors: Tamer I. A. Abudabbousa, Vanjari Venkata Ramana, Sudarshan Kumar Babu Valluru
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The PV module output power depends on the environmental conditions under which it works. Mismatching conditions, which occur when the environmental conditions are distributed unevenly on the module's surface, significantly affect the module's behavior and can lead to power losses. Several methods are presented to maximize power generation from a PV module under mismatching conditions. Module-level reconfiguration, the process of modifying the submodules interconnection, has proven effective in enhancing power extraction. This paper presents an easy-to-implement modification to a commercial PV module that enables dynamic reconfiguration of its submodules, along with a technique for determining the optimal submodule configuration. A buck converter is proposed for efficient power conversion. An evaluation of the presented system was carried out by comparing its module output power and load power with those of a conventional system under various environmental conditions. Under the proposed mismatching conditions, the module power improvement of the presented system ranges between 9.26% and 46.51%. Furthermore, the load power of the presented system is increased by 8.19% to 48.15%.

title: AI-Powered Skin Lesion Detection and Classification using Emerging Techniques
authors: D. Ferlin Deva Shahila, D. Lilly Evangelin, Valantina Stephen, Babitha Lincy R, Berakhah.F.Stanley, Yeswanth Hanumanthu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Deep learning-based skin lesion categorization has become a viable method for automated dermatological diagnosis and prognosis. Through the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and other deep learning facsimile, researchers have made significant progress in correctly identifying different kinds of skin lesions from digital photos. An overview of current advancements and approaches in deep learning-based skin lesion categorization is provided in this research. We talk about the difficulties in acquiring datasets, annotating them, and optimizing models. We also go over how to integrate multimodal data sources to improve classification accuracy. The usefulness and drawbacks of deep learning-based methods for classifying skin lesions are demonstrated through an analysis of a number of case studies and research techniques. Additionally, covered are ethical issues including algorithmic bias and patient privacy. This work attempts to give insights into the present state-of-the-art in automated skin lesion categorization using deep learning and identify future research paths in the subject by doing a exhaustive assessment gauge of the literature. Obtaining and annotating high-quality information is one of the main obstacles in the categorization of skin lesions. In order to guarantee the resilience and generalizability of deep learning methods, researchers have investigated a number of datasets gathering and annotation methodologies. Achieving the best classification performance also heavily relies on model optimization strategies, such as architecture design and hyperparameter tweaking. The use of multimodal data sources, including histopathological findings, clinical history, and meatoscopic pictures, has demonstrated encouraging outcomes in improving classification accuracy. Deep learning models can offer more thorough and precise diagnostic insights by integrating complimentary data from many modalities. Dermatologists may be able to increase the precision and effectiveness of skin lesion diagnosis by utilizing these methods, which might result in prompt treatment and improved patient outcomes. Deep learning-based methods present a viable way to increase dermatologists' capacity to identify skin cancer, which will finally lead to good patient care and healthcare delivery.

title: Edge-AI and Multimodal Sensor Fusion–Based Smart Stick for Visually Impaired Individuals with Real-Time Obstacle Detection
authors: Dhivya P, C. N. Vanitha, Suryavisanth R S, Sujith S, Joel Seba Clitues J, Sureshkumar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Independent mobility for visually impaired individuals is often limited by the inability of conventional assistive tools to provide early and reliable awareness of surrounding obstacles. While recent smart navigation aids incorporate sensing and artificial intelligence, many existing solutions depend on cloud computation or single-modality sensing, resulting in latency, privacy concerns, and reduced robustness in real-world environments. This work proposes a fully on-device smart stick based on Edge Artificial Intelligence and multimodal sensor fusion for real-time obstacle detection. A lightweight TinyML-based convolutional neural network processes visual input on a XIAO ESP32S3 microcontroller, while ultrasonic and infrared sensors supply complementary distance and proximity information. These modalities are combined using a decision-level fusion strategy to enhance detection reliability under varying environmental conditions. Experimental validation conducted in indoor and semi-outdoor settings demonstrates an average detection accuracy of 0.89, with precision and recall values of 0.88 and 0.86, respectively. The system achieves inference latency below 125 ms and operates with low power consumption suitable for prolonged use. The results confirm the feasibility of deploying multimodal Edge-AI perception on resource-constrained assistive devices.

title: Degradation Aware Adaptive Charging Strategies for Ultra-Fast Electric Vehicle Charging
authors: K Lakshmi Prasad Reddy, M. Vijaya Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Ultra-fast charging (UFC) is essential for improving the usability of electric vehicles (EVs); however, aggressive charging currents significantly accelerate battery degradation and electro-thermal stress, adversely impacting safety and lifetime. This paper presents a degradation-aware adaptive charging strategy for ultra-fast EV charging that systematically translates battery electro-thermal degradation insights into practical charging control. A physics-informed battery model incorporating state-of-charge (SoC) dynamics, electro-thermal behavior, and state-of-health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> degradation is employed. The proposed strategy dynamically regulates the charging current based on SoC and battery temperature, without relying on learning-based optimization. The charging framework is evaluated for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 0\ V}, \mathbf{1 8 0\ A h\ E V}$</tex> battery pack under ultra-fast charging conditions with charging currents up to 350 A over a 3600 s charging horizon. Performance is compared against conventional constant-current-constant-voltage (CC-CV) and multistage charging strategies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed degradationaware strategy significantly reduces electro-thermal stress and battery degradation. Specifically, the peak normalized battery temperature is reduced from 2691.8 (CC-CV) and 1975.6 (multistage) to 880.6, corresponding to a 67.3 % reduction relative to CC-CV charging. Moreover, cumulative degradation is reduced by more than 36 orders of magnitude, from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.9 \times 10^{58}$</tex> under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C C - C V}$</tex> charging to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 9} \times \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{2 2}}$</tex> using the proposed strategy. Although the proposed method increases charging time to 3600 s, compared to 1975 s for CC-CV charging, it establishes an effective trade-off between charging speed and battery health preservation. The results confirm that embedding degradation awareness into charging control substantially improves battery longevity and thermal safety, and provides a systematic transition toward learning-based optimal charging strategies for ultra-fast EV applications.

title: Model-Less Forecast-Proactive Intelligent Control for Inverter in Islanded Microgrid
authors: Mustafa A. Kamoona, Juan Manuel Mauricio
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Islanded inverter-dominated microgrids with high penetration of renewable energy sources face significant voltage regulation challenges under unbalanced and time-varying load conditions. Conventional voltage controllers, such as proportional-integral-derivative (PID) schemes, rely on accurate system modeling and careful parameter tuning, which limits their adaptability under uncertain operating conditions. This paper proposes a model-less forecast-proactive Reinforcement Learning (RL) voltage control framework for islanded AC microgrids supplied through a single DC source and grid-forming inverter. The proposed controller directly adjusts the inverter voltage reference using only local measurements, without requiring an explicit network model. A simple non-learning forecast feature based on the next-hour load schedule is incorporated to improve anticipatory control actions during peak demand periods. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated on a fourbus islanded AC microgrid with unbalanced and dynamic loads. Comparative studies against a model-based PID controller and an LSTM-based baseline demonstrate that the proposed RL controller achieves voltage regulation performance comparable to optimally tuned PID control while maintaining strict voltage limits of 0.98-1.02 pu. The results confirm that the proposed model-less framework provides a practical and robust alternative for intelligent voltage control in islanded microgrids.

title: Investigating the Impact of Dimensionality Reduction on Spectral Unmixing Accuracy Using NIST-Traceable Colour Calibration Standard
authors: Ondřej Vaculík, Mojmír Šerý, Lukáš Šilhan, Denisa Šilhanová, Pavel Zemánek
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This study explores the impact of dimensionality reduction (DR) on spectral unmixing accuracy using a National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) traceable colour transmission calibration slide. Hyperspectral images were acquired and analysed alongside high-accuracy NIST reference spectral transmission data. The used analysis pipeline integrates DR techniques (principal component analysis, non-negative matrix factorisation, and autoencoder neural networks) with spectral unmixing algorithms (non-negative least squares and multivariate curve resolution). Reconstruction accuracy was evaluated using root mean squared error and spectral angle distance. The findings indicate that low-dimensional representations effectively yield low spectral reconstruction error. In particular, the combination of non-negative matrix factorisation with three components and multivariate curve resolution achieved the lowest reconstruction error. This work demonstrates that integrating DR with spectral unmixing enables reliable colour patch composition estimation, balancing data compression, and analytical accuracy.

title: Open-Set Domain Generalization for Hyperspectral Image Classification via Evidential Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Amirreza Khoshbakht, Erchan Aptoula
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Open-set domain generalization for hyperspectral image classification tackles challenges from both unknown classes and cross-domain generalization without target adaptation. We propose a framework that extracts domain-agnostic features through attention-weighted frequency analysis, while capturing complementary spectral-spatial information via parallel pathways. It provides reliable open-set classification through uncertainty-aware pathway weighting. Experiments on Pavia University and Centre indicate performance levels comparable to state-of-the-art domain adaptation methods despite not requiring target domain access during training.

title: Low-Area Fused Floating-Point Four-Term Adder
authors: Thiruvenkadam Krishnan, Parthibaraj Anguraj, Barkunan SR, Saravanan Subramanian, Vivek Karthick Perumal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Floating-point (FP) logic is used in the computation field. Among different operations, FP addition has been studied more in recent years. This paper deals with an area-efficient architecture for Fused FP Four-Term (FFPF-T) addition. In the proposed design, a modified exponent processing unit and a new Significand Alignment (SA) block are used. A simple adder is applied for Mantissa Addition (MA) to perform the computation. The existing method is extended to support FFPF-T addition. The efficiency of the designs is evaluated using the Altera Quartus II simulation tool. The implementation outcome indicates that the proposed design requires less hardware area compared to the existing schemes.

title: Li Ion Life Estimation System using Machine Learning
authors: Sarathkumar R, Fathima Shirin M, Haririthanyaa S, Jayaprakash S, Premnath S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent Battery Management System (BMS) designed to enhance the performance and lifespan of lithium-ion batteries through the integration of machine learning techniques. The proposed system leverages real-time sensor data to predict key battery parameters such as State of Health (SOH) and State of Charge (SOC), enabling proactive fault detection and optimized energy management. Utilizing microcontrollers and analog-to-digital converters, sensor data including temperature, voltage, and current is continuously monitored and processed. A machine learning model trained on historical battery data identifies anomalies and adjusts charging and discharging operations dynamically. Additionally, the system incorporates self-healing mechanisms that respond to early-stage faults, ensuring safety and system stability. Experimental validation demonstrates the model's accuracy and the system’s effectiveness under varied load conditions. The proposed solution supports predictive maintenance strategies, reduces operational risks, and aligns with the growing demand for efficient and sustainable battery technologies. This work contributes to the development of reliable, AI-driven BMS solutions for applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage systems.

title: Design and Fabrication of CSRR based Multi-Port Antenna System for 5G Smart Phone Devices
authors: Alamelu Alias Rajasree S, Kalaimathi B, Adline Jancy J, Shri Lakshmi S, Swathiga A, Vinodha N
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The performance of future wireless communication systems largely relies on the behavior of technologies like Massive MIMO in 5G and future networks. The antenna is designed on a 75 × 150 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> thick 1.5 mm FR4 substrate. An 8×8 MIMO antenna system is optimized within the frequency range of 3.1 GHz to 3.6 GHz with a resonant frequency at 3.4 GHz. Simulation and analysis is carried out employing Ansys HFSS, analyzing the most critical performance parameters including S-parameters, gain, efficiency, directivity, VSWR, Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) below 0.2, Diversity Gain (DG) of 10dB. Simulation results reveal the maximum gain to be 4.57 dB, directivity of 5.23 dB, and return loss of -25 dB at resonant frequency of 3.4 GHz. The antenna has a high radiation efficiency of 87.38%, thus ensuring efficient transmission of power with low losses. The proposed design has better radiation properties, reduced mutual coupling, and superior isolation between antenna elements. These properties together render the antenna a good candidate to be integrated into future wireless communication systems.

title: AI-Enabled IOT Health Monitoring with Multi-Vital Sensing and Location-Triggered Emergency Alert Mechanism
authors: Aarav Singhal, Prasenjit Sarkar, Saumy Singh, S. Latha, P. Muthu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In today's scenario, due to the increasing burden of chronic diseases, the aging population, and the limitations of periodic health checkups, the demand for health monitoring has increased drastically. Health monitoring in conventional healthcare systems is done through observation, which is not effective in detecting changes in the body. Hence, the development of intelligent health monitoring systems is highly required. This is the challenge addressed in the proposed Health Monitoring System, which combines wearable sensor technology for real-time health monitoring with machine learning analytics. These include data on essential physiological parameters (heart rate, body temperature, oxygen saturation, motion) which are acquired by embedded sensors, which are processed via an Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) model. Unlike other machine learning models, the EBM model offers high predictive accuracy and interpretability that allow easy interpretation of how each physiological parameter affects health risk prediction. It classifies health conditions into predefined risk categories and presents explanations and predictions together which augments trust and clinical usability. Integration of IoT allows for real-time data transmission and remote monitoring through a connected interface as a preventive measure when abnormal conditions are detected. Coupled with sensor-based data acquisition, explainable machine learning and remote access, the proposed system can be applied for continuous health monitoring with reliable, explainable and scalable systems. This not only advances early detection and decision-making but also is a major step forward for patient-centric intelligent health systems.

title: Scalable Artificial Intelligence Models for Big Data Processing and Analysis
authors: Surya Veera Brahmaji Rao Sunnam
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Scalable artificial intelligence (AI) models have emerged as a pivotal solution for processing and analyzing large-scale datasets in a variety of domains, including healthcare, finance, and e-commerce. With the advent of big data, the need for intelligent systems capable of handling vast amounts of unstructured and structured data has increased exponentially. This paper explores the integration of scalable AI models with big data frameworks to address challenges such as data volume, velocity, and variety. Key methods for improving scalability in AI models, such as distributed learning, parallel processing, and cloud-based systems, are discussed. Furthermore, the paper highlights the role of advanced algorithms like deep learning, reinforcement learning, and ensemble methods in managing big data. A focus is placed on how these AI techniques can optimize data processing, enhance predictive analytics, and provide valuable insights for decision-making. Additionally, the paper reviews case studies demonstrating the application of scalable AI models in real-world big data environments and discusses future directions in the evolution of AI models for big data analytics.

title: Intelligent Robotic Waste Segregation using YOLOv11 for Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Doha Masrour, Yassine Salih-Alj
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: With the rapid increase in global waste generation, efficient segregation remains a critical challenge due to limited automation and reliance on manual labor. This paper presents the design and development of an intelligent waste segregation system that integrates a six-degree-of-freedom (6DOF) robotic manipulator with the YOLOv11 Nano object detection algorithm for real-time classification and sorting. The YOLOv11 Nano network is trained to identify seven waste categories: paper, plastic, metal, glass, cardboard, and textile, with optimized inference speed and detection accuracy. The robotic arm, modeled using Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) parameters, executes inverse kinematic control for precise pick-and-place operations. By integrating the state-of-the-art YOLOv11 Nano vision-based detection model with a fully articulated 6DOF robotic manipulator, the proposed design enhances the automation, accuracy, and safety of waste segregation operations.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Using Meteorological Data
authors: Adhin Thomas, Mohamad Nassereddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power generation is inherently dependent on meteorological conditions, making accurate day-ahead forecasting critical for scheduling, reserve management, and grid stability. However, many existing machine learning (ML) approaches rely on limited meteorological integration or evaluate such inputs in an ad-hoc manner, resulting in sub-optimal performance for longer forecasting horizons. This paper investigates a hybrid deep learning approach for day-ahead PV forecasting that integrates multiple meteorological variables within a single production-feasible architecture. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model cooperatively capture structured relationships between meteorological inputs and temporal PV dynamics. The key contribution is the systematic evaluation of individual meteorological variables irradiance, air pressure, wind speed, temperature, humidity, and cloud cover within a unified hybrid ML framework, addressing the gap where such variables are often included without explicit contribution analysis. Experimental results show that meteorological augmentation improves forecasting accuracy compared to persistence, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM-only baselines, while sensitivity analysis provides actionable insight into the relative operational value of each variable, supporting informed sensor deployment in practical PV applications.

title: Decentralized Deepfake Detection: Feature-Aligned Federated Learning with Multi-Backbone Fusion
authors: R. Geetha, Jayanthi. V, G.R.Priyasri, P. Geetha, Kavitha. T
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Digital security besides media forensics now face a noteworthy difficulty in detecting fabricated faces due to the spread of deepfake technologies and facial forgery techniques. In order to identify face forgeries while protecting user privacy, this research presents a new-fangled framework named FFDL, which stands for Feature Fusion- Deep Learning utilising Federated Learning. This approach creates a privacy-aware, decentralised detection scheme by combining federated learning (FL) multi-source feature fusion. The model extracts strong visual features on client side by utilising the Inception-v3 and Xception architectures. Prior to being fed into a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier, these characteristics are fused using Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA) to boost their correlation besides discriminative capacities. By keeping all raw data inside the client environment during model training with the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) protocol, can guarantee that your data will remain private. In validate the experiments using FaceForensics++ and WildDeepfake for cross-dataset generalisation, Deepforensics-1.0 dataset was used. Outperforming both traditional centralised and non-fused models, the suggested FFDL model attained a peak accuracy of 98.99%, precision of 98.80%, recall of 98.85%, besides F1-score of 98.82%. Results from ablation studies validated the importance of each component, besides model's consistency in performance across new datasets proved its durability. In addition, the training and validation AUC-ROC scores showed that the classification capability was almost flawless. The efficacy of federated, fused-feature deep learning in attaining accuracy, scalability, and privacy is demonstrated in this paper. -supporting the identification of fake faces, a crucial step in the development of trustworthy methods of authenticating media and verifying identities in practical settings.

title: Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy using Intelligent Gradient Boosted Decision Trees
authors: M. Sharmila, K G.Sathiya, G.Uma, R Hemalatha, P.Pavithra, R.Suresh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), a progressive eye disease brought on by long-term diabetes, can lead to blindness or visual impairment if left undiagnosed. Due to minor retinal changes, image variability, as well as limited access to experts, timely and accurate detection is essential but challenging. Efficient screening necessitates the adoption of effective, automated, as well as easily comprehensible diagnostic techniques. Hence, this research is developed to perform the DR detection using intelligent machine learning methodology. The dataset is first gathered from the online sources called Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography (OCTA) India Dataset. This gathered data undergoes the preprocessing by the Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) + Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) approaches. The extraction of the features from these pre-processed data is done by the Visual Geometry Group (VGG19) approach. The detection of DR is done finally by the intelligent machine learning model referred as Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDT). The proposed GBDT-DR model in terms of accuracy and recall is 5.51% and 6.90% better than the considered existing methods for the DR detection model respectively.

title: GLREM-Net: Groundnut Leaf Disease Detection using Deep Learning based ResMobileNet
authors: Suganya.J, Gethzi Jeba Leelipushpam Paulraj, Immanuel Johnraja Jebadurai, Jebaveerasingh Jebadurai
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Groundnuts are an important crop on a global scale and are suffering from leaf diseases that are reducing yields considerably. To ensure continuous agricultural productivity, it is crucial to identify these diseases as soon as possible. Manual detection of plant diseases is a challenging and time-consuming task that requires expert knowledge and continuous field monitoring. Early detection is crucial to prevent the spread of diseases and to minimize crop loss. However, most existing systems are not capable of identifying diseases at their early stages, which limits their effectiveness in practical agricultural scenarios. To address these challenges, a novel model named GLREM-NET, based on the Residual Mobile Network (ResMobileNet) architecture, is proposed for accurate and efficient early-stage detection of groundnut leaf diseases. Initially, the input groundnut leaf disease (GLD) images are preprocessed using the Trilateral Filter (TF) to improve the quality of the images. The pre-processed images are fed into the Residual Mobile Network (ResMobileNet) for extracting the fine features. Finally, the Deep Neural Network (DNN) is used to classify the ground leaf disease into normal or abnormal. The proposed DNN model is evaluated based on its F1 score (F1S), specificity (SP), precision (PS), recall (RC), and accuracy (AC). The classification accuracy of 99.87% for the proposed GLREM-NET is highly reliable for the ground leaf disease dataset. The proposed GLREM-NET for groundnut leaf disease detection model achieves an overall accuracy of 0.18%, 1.9%, 1.43%, and 3.4% compared to existing methods such as Inception-V4, ResNet50, DenseNet169, and CNN, respectively.

title: Oncoverse: A Multi-Agent Conversational System for Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Sharanya Acharya, Yami Sunil, Poshika Reddy, Padmavathy T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of breast cancer is essential, along with clinical understanding to facilitate prompt decision-making. This research introduces a multi-agent dialogue diagnostic system that combines deep learning, knowledge graphs, and reasoning generated by large language models (LLMs) via an adaptive confidence fusion approach. The suggested system includes a Coordinator Agent that manages workflow, an Imaging Agent utilizing a DenseNet CNN for classifying breast ultrasound images, and a Reasoning Agent that enhances predictions with knowledge-graph evidence and LLM-driven dialogue for diagnostic reasoning. In contrast to traditional single-model pipelines, our method adaptively modifies decision weights through an Adaptive Fusion Module that merges imaging confidence, reasoning dependability, and semantic medical connections to produce a well-calibrated diagnosis with justification and a BI-RADS-compliant risk score. Experimental results demonstrate that the adaptive fusion framework achieves superior performance compared to imaging-only and fixed-rule methods, reaching an overall accuracy of 89.84 % with high sensitivity and precision across benign, malignant, and normal classes, thereby validating its effectiveness as a robust and interpretable diagnostic assistant.

title: Organizational Efficiency: The Role of SHRP and Technology Integration in Driving Performance
authors: Belal Shneikat, Khaled M. Alhyasat, Hamza Elrehail, Sharon Mendoza-Dreisbach, Abdallah Mohammad Ta’amneh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study focuses on SHRP and technology integration as the source of organizational efficiency and performance in the scenario of rapidly changing organizational environment, especially in digitally driven sectors. This research uses a quantitative research design aimed at the Information Technology (IT) industry in Abu Dhabi, UAE, where organizations are actively undergoing digital transformation and complex workforce situations. A structured online questionnaire was used to collect data, which were analyzed using SmartPLS on 290 valid responses to investigate direct, indirect and mediating relationships. Results showed that SHRP, technology integration and organizational efficiency had positive and statistically significant relationships. Additionally, SHRP was found to moderate the relationship with efficiency through technology integration, and the synergy is obvious. The strong implications of these findings for HR leaders and organizational strategists are that HR investing in digital HR systems and collaboration between the HR and IT functions and the adoption of integrated strategic planning practices are necessary for higher performance outcomes. What the study points out is that organizational efficiency is not any more determined by individual practices but requires a coordinated strategy based on the combined use of human capital and technology as interdependent assets.

title: BERT-Electra Synergistic Model with Cross-Attention for Enhanced Fake News Detection
authors: Lakshitha Shree R M, Vishnu Kumar Kaliappan, Dharani Jaganathan, Pavithra P, Kaviya Sree S, Sarmili M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The quick dissemination of digital misinformation has turned fake news detection into a vital challenge that alters the perception of information and the public's opinion. The paper introduces a Dual-Stream Transformer architecture that brings together BERT and ELECTRA in order to seize not only the high-level semantic context but also the fine-grained token-level irregularities that are often found in the texts. BERT captures the global contextual coherence while ELECTRA identifies the lexical anomalies and they both work together through a Cross-Attention Fusion Layer that combines their representations to classify news articles effectively in binary terms. The conducted experiments using benchmark datasets highlight that the suggested model is always better than the traditional machine learning and hybrid BERT-based methods considering the metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Its modular and scalable architecture allows for real-time deployment and even future extensions, that include but are not limited to, multimodal, multilingual, and explainable AI enhancements.
